name: Almanzora Valley Greenway Purchena Loop
name_original: Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora — circular desde Purchena
description: This difficult 17.6-mile touring cycling route from Purchena offers varied terrain and panoramic views of the Almanzora Valley.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Purchena traverse the diverse landscapes of the Almanzora Valley, situated at the foot of the Sierra de los Filabres. The region features a network of cycling-friendly paths, including segments of the Vía Verde del Almanzora, built on an old railway line. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from mostly flat, well-signposted greenways to more challenging routes ascending into the surrounding mountains. Historical elements such as old viaducts and tunnels are integrated into the cycling experience. Best touring cycling routes around Purchena The most popular touring cycling route is Sierro and Almanzora Greenway Loop, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of historic castles. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Almanzora Valley Greenway Purchena Loop, a difficult 17.6 miles (28.3 km) path. This route provides varied terrain and extensive views across the Almanzora Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Cantoria Tunnel – Almanzora loop from Estación de Fines-Olula, a 25.5 miles (41.1 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde del Almanzora, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Purchena is defined by the Almanzora Valley, the Sierra de los Filabres, and the Vía Verde, offering a mix of flat greenways and mountain ascents. The network provides options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to more demanding excursions. The routes in Purchena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 13 reviews. More than 270 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Purchena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Purchena?
A: There are over 45 touring cycling routes around Purchena, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy greenways to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Purchena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Purchena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 13 reviews. More than 270 touring cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views of the Almanzora Valley and the well-maintained Vía Verde sections.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Purchena?
A: Purchena offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant weather with mild temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in the valley, while winters are typically mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though some higher mountain routes might be cooler.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Purchena?
A: Yes, Purchena is home to several easy touring cycling routes, including sections of the Vía Verde del Almanzora. These routes are mostly flat, well-signposted, and built on old railway lines, making them ideal for beginners and families. They allow cyclists of all ages and fitness levels to enjoy the tranquil rural environment. For a family-friendly option, consider routes that stick to the Vía Verde.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes in Purchena?
A: Absolutely. While the Vía Verde offers easier rides, Purchena also serves as a base for more demanding excursions into the surrounding mountains, particularly the Sierra de los Filabres. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1039194679/almanzora-valley-greenway-purchena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanzora Valley Greenway Purchena Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer varied terrain and significant elevation changes, providing a good challenge for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Purchena area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Purchena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047498286/sierro-and-almanzora-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierro and Almanzora Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of historic castles and returns you to your starting point.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on a touring cycling route around Purchena?
A: Touring cycling around Purchena offers diverse landscapes. You'll traverse the fertile Almanzora Valley with its ancient olive groves, follow the Vía Verde through rural environments, and ascend into the greener Sierra de los Filabres with its forests and streams. Panoramic views of the valley and surrounding mountains are common, especially from viewpoints like Silveria.

Q: Are there dog-friendly touring cycling options in Purchena?
A: While the Vía Verde del Almanzora is generally accessible, it's always best to check local regulations regarding dogs on specific sections or within natural parks. Many outdoor areas in the region are dog-friendly, but keeping your dog on a leash and ensuring they are comfortable with the distance and terrain is advisable.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints along the cycling routes in Purchena?
A: The region boasts several scenic viewpoints. The Silveria Viewpoint and Leisure Zone in the Sierra de los Filabres offers panoramic vistas of Purchena, the Almanzora Valley, and the surrounding mountain ranges. Additionally, routes often provide expansive views of the valley, especially those that climb higher. You might also encounter highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4625289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Piedra Lobera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while cycling around Purchena?
A: Purchena is rich in history. Along your cycling routes, you can encounter the remains of the Alcazaba de Purchena, including the notable Torre del Agua. The Vía Verde del Almanzora itself features historical elements like old viaducts, tunnels, and repurposed railway stations such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4807759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serón Railway Station&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fines-Olula Station&lt;/a&gt;, which offer glimpses into the area's railway past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct near Purchena&lt;/a&gt; is another interesting landmark.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the cycling routes in Purchena?
A: Purchena and the towns along the Vía Verde del Almanzora offer various amenities. You'll find cafes and restaurants in the villages, some even located in old railway stations along the greenway. Accommodation options, including hotels and rural guesthouses, are available in Purchena and nearby towns, catering to cyclists looking for a comfortable stay.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Purchena?
A: Parking is generally available in Purchena town center and in the villages along the Vía Verde del Almanzora. Look for designated parking areas near the start points of popular routes or public parking facilities within the towns. Many of the old railway stations that serve as access points to the Vía Verde also offer parking.
tips: If you are traveling along the Vía Verde from west to east in a descending direction, this aqueduct announces your arrival at the old Purchena station.

I recommend a visit to this museum. More than 1,200 pieces of contemporary art by Spanish authors are on display.
The large woman's head will mark its location from afar.

It is a pity that this station, which was once one of the most important on the line, has not been maintained and restored. Even so, pedaling through here evokes the importance that the railway once had in the entire area.

I recommend that you invest a little while in visiting the town and its curious square dedicated to the moon. You can also recharge water and energy to continue the Route, if you are following the Greenway.

faqs.question: Is this route truly circular, and what is its difficulty?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora itself is a linear, easy path, this specific komoot route is designed as a circular touring bicycle journey starting and ending in Purchena. It covers 28.3 km with an elevation gain of 491 meters, making it rated as difficult. This difficulty comes from combining sections of the Vía Verde with other local roads or trails that involve more significant climbs and descents than the Vía Verde's typically gentle gradients.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route combines sections of the Vía Verde, which features mostly compacted earth, gravel, and some asphalt, with other local roads or trails. The Vía Verde portions are generally flat and well-signposted, but the overall circular route includes varied terrain and elevation changes that contribute to its 'difficult' rating.

faqs.question: What interesting sights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several points of interest. Near Purchena, you can see the Aqueduct near Purchena and explore the town of Purchena itself. The route also passes by the Fines-Olula Station and the Casa Ibáñez Museum in Olula del Río. You'll also enjoy panoramic views of the Almanzora Valley and historical railway structures like old viaducts.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route in Purchena?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Purchena is the starting and ending point for this circular route. You should be able to find parking within the town, likely near the Vía Verde access points or in municipal parking areas. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point?
faqs.answer: Information on specific public transport links to Purchena for trail access is not readily available. It's best to check local bus schedules or regional transport options if you plan to arrive without a private vehicle.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for cycling with my dog?
faqs.answer: Vías Verdes in Spain are generally dog-friendly, but specific leash rules can vary. Given that this route combines the Vía Verde with other roads, it's recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially on road sections or near populated areas, for their safety and the safety of others.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Almanzora Valley can get very hot in summer. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with comfortable weather and beautiful scenery. Winter can also be a good option, though it might be cooler.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to use this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to cycle on the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora or the public roads that make up this circular route.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' rating, this specific circular route from Purchena, with its 28.3 km distance and 491 meters of elevation gain, might be challenging for beginners or young children. While sections of the Vía Verde itself are easy and family-friendly, the full circular loop requires a good level of fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route in Purchena?
faqs.answer: Yes, Purchena is a town where you can find local amenities. You should be able to find cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options to refuel or stay overnight before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates a significant portion of the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora (approximately 39% of the route). It also briefly touches upon sections of the Antigua linea Murcia-Granada and the Rambla de Olula.
